claim: Extracting high-level system architecture from codebases is the key alpha for code indexing; LLMs can read files but need explicit architecture extraction to understand systems
actionable: When building code RAG systems, explicitly extract and visualize system architecture separate from file contents; include service boundaries and data flow

claim: Multiple graph representations of codebases are useful for understanding: folder structure, language server graphs, and commit history graphs reveal different ownership and evolution patterns
actionable: Build code understanding systems using multiple graph types (FST, LSP, commit history); combine signals for better architecture extraction

claim: Dynamic linking (string-based runtime invocation) is the hardest part of code structure inference; static analysis tools cannot determine which systems are coupled at runtime
actionable: When indexing codebases without execution, expect gaps in understanding dynamic dispatch; flag uncertain system boundaries for manual review

claim: Indexing ~30,000 repos with 4 billion lines of code cost $300k in compute; scaling to millions of repos will be extremely expensive and may not reach economic equilibrium quickly
actionable: Budget aggressively for code indexing infrastructure; consider incremental updates (only on PRs for paid tiers) to manage costs

claim: Auto-refresh on free tiers is infeasible at scale; cost-effective solution: detect if repo has wiki badge and only auto-update those (100k+ repos), creating alignment between users and costs
actionable: Design free tier incentives to reduce compute costs; reward user participation (badges) with automatic refresh; avoid subsidizing all users equally

claim: Code embedding models for RAG are a hard unsolved problem; no clear best-in-class solution exists yet, requiring custom evaluation and curation
actionable: If building code RAG systems, invest in custom code embedding evaluations; don't assume off-the-shelf embeddings work well for code

claim: Repository setup is one of the hardest parts of agentic code work; environments require VPN, keys, databases (Redis, Postgres), and undocumented networking setup
actionable: Before deploying agents to onboard repos, solve environment setup automation; this is a harder problem than most development automation

claim: Dev containers are underadopted despite being the closest standard for reproducible development environments; adoption is nearly zero and developers are skeptical
actionable: Don't rely on dev containers for automation; expect to build custom environment setup logic for each major framework and tech stack

claim: Deep research on code (multi-step reasoning vs instant search) is most valuable for exploration and traversing related systems; simple searches handle direct lookups better
actionable: Implement both fast and deep search modes in code tools; use fast for direct queries, deep reasoning for system exploration and architectural understanding
